name: Serena Williams
executiveSummary:
Serena Williams is an American former professional tennis player, widely regarded as one of the greatest athletes of all time. She won 23 Grand Slam women's singles titles, the most in the Open Era, and the second-most of all time.

Beyond her unprecedented success on the court, Williams is a prolific investor through her firm Serena Ventures, focusing on diverse founders, and is an advocate for women's rights and maternal health.
biography:
Serena Williams was born in Saginaw, Michigan, and raised in Compton, California, where she began playing tennis at age four under the coaching of her parents, Richard Williams and Oracene Price. She turned professional in 1995 and won her first major singles title at the 1999 US Open.

Over the next two decades, Williams dominated women's tennis, holding the Women's Tennis Association (WTA) world No. 1 ranking in singles on eight separate occasions between 2002 and 2017. She achieved the rare 'Serena Slam' twice—holding all four major singles titles simultaneously. Alongside her sister Venus, she also won 14 major women's doubles titles and four Olympic gold medals.

She 'evolved away' from professional tennis after the 2022 US Open to focus on her family and her investment firm, Serena Ventures, which has invested in dozens of startups. She is also a successful fashion designer and author.

recentNews.title: Serena Ventures Expansion
recentNews.description: Actively investing in diverse startups and AI technology companies.

recentNews.title: Publishing Deal
recentNews.description: Announced the upcoming publication of her memoirs and additional books.
